records.ety: [Gr. <?/ vapor (akin to Skr. ātman breath, soul, G. athem breath) + <?/ sphere: cf. F. atmosphre. See Sphere.]

defs: The whole mass of aëriform fluid surrounding the earth; -- applied also to the gaseous envelope of any celestial orb, or other body; as, the atmosphere of Mars.
A supposed medium around various bodies; as, electrical atmosphere, a medium formerly supposed to surround electrical bodies.
The pressure or weight of the air at the sea level, on a unit of surface, or about 14.7 lbs. to the sq. inch.
quotes: Hydrogen was liquefied under a pressure of 650 atmospheres.

defs: Any surrounding or pervading influence or condition.
The portion of air in any locality, or affected by a special physical or sanitary condition; as, the atmosphere of the room; a moist or noxious atmosphere.
